$comment: Registry of browsers/OSes ported to the web via v86 (WASM CPU + canvas + Wisp networking). Image URLs are relative to the CDN host (default https://i.copy.sh/, overridable with ?cdn=). The `config` object mirrors v86 constructor options; the runner prepends the CDN host to every disk/state `url`.

browsers.name: KolibriOS
browsers.blurb: Tiny assembly-written OS that boots from a single 1.44 MB floppy in seconds. Ships a real graphical web browser (WebView) with TCP/IP networking.
browsers.hint: Open WEBVIEW (or NETSURF) from the desktop to start browsing.

browsers.name: Windows 2000
browsers.blurb: Microsoft Windows 2000 Professional resumed from a saved state, with the real Internet Explorer 5 / Trident engine. Also bundles K-Meleon (Gecko), Lynx and Retrozilla. Networked through Wisp.
browsers.hint: First double-click 'networking.bat' on the desktop, then open Internet Explorer (or K-Meleon / Lynx / Retrozilla).

browsers.name: Windows 98
browsers.blurb: Windows 98 resumed from a saved state with Internet Explorer. A widely-used browser of its era running entirely in your tab.
browsers.hint: First double-click 'networking.bat' on the desktop, then open Internet Explorer.

browsers.name: Windows ME
browsers.blurb: Windows Millennium Edition resumed from a saved state, with Internet Explorer.
browsers.hint: Enable networking (look for 'networking.bat' on the desktop), then open Internet Explorer.

browsers.name: Haiku
browsers.blurb: Haiku is a modern reimagining of BeOS. Its native browser, WebPositive, is built on WebKit, so it renders the modern web. Also includes the Links browser. Resumed from a saved state.
browsers.hint: In the Terminal, run 'networking.sh' to enable networking, then open WebPositive.

browsers.name: ReactOS
browsers.blurb: ReactOS is an open-source OS binary-compatible with Windows. Boots from a saved state to a familiar desktop. Networked through Wisp.
browsers.hint: Open a web browser from the Start menu; enable networking if prompted.

browsers.name: SerenityOS
browsers.blurb: SerenityOS ships Ladybird, a from-scratch independent browser engine (LibWeb/LibJS). Resumed from a saved state.
browsers.hint: Open Ladybird (or Browser) from the taskbar.

browsers.name: Damn Small Linux
browsers.blurb: A 50 MB Linux live CD that bundles the Dillo and Firefox browsers. Boots into X11 from CD-ROM.
browsers.hint: It boots automatically; once the X11 desktop appears, launch Dillo or Firefox from the desktop / menu.
